pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 13-15: PROJECT_DIR is set to '/home/ga/scheduler-bot' and directory is created with mkdir -p
docker-compose.yml line 82: 'container_name: scheduler-bot' explicitly defined; also referenced in export_result.sh line 10
task.json line 19-20 defines target_timezone as 'America/New_York'; verifier.py line 64 explicitly checks for 'New_York' in env_tz and EST/EDT in date output
bot.py line 38: 'user_name = "Raphaël"' is hard-coded in the Python application; export_result.sh line 68 explicitly searches logs for 'Raphaël' to verify Unicode handling

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic environment with a Python bot application located at /home/ga/scheduler-bot running in a Docker container named 'scheduler-bot'. The task requires fixing timezone to America/New_York (EST/EDT timezone) and locale configuration to handle UTF-8 characters. The application processes a user named Raphaël (containing non-ASCII character ë) and must display this name correctly in logs. Success requires installing tzdata package on Alpine, setting TZ=America/New_York environment variable, setting LANG to UTF-8 locale (e.g., C.UTF-8 or en_US.UTF-8), and verifying that both the container date command returns EST/EDT and the logs show successful Unicode processing.
